title: CoreWeave-Tied Data Center Seeks $850 Million Junk Bond Sale
detail:
CoreWeave-Tied Data Center Seeks $850 Million Junk Bond Sale
Gowri Gurumurthy and Michelle Cheng
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 11:28 AM MST 2 min read
CRWV
META
MSFT
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

(Bloomberg) -- A data center tied to CoreWeave Inc. is set to get funding from a $850 million junk-bond sale by Elk Grove Village Property LLC, which joins a wave of high-yield issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

Most Read from Bloomberg

US Says Deals With Iran for Safe Hormuz Transit Are Prohibited

Berkshire Hathaway to Buy Taylor Morrison for $6.8 Billion

A Rare ‘Super’ El Niño Is Looking More Likely. Here’s What to Expect

Russia Finance Officials Tell Putin War Spending Is Unaffordable

Stocks Up as Trump Cites Rapid Pace in Iran Talks: Markets Wrap

Elk Grove, a wholly-owned indirect subsidiary of Prime Data Centers LLC, is developing a “build-to-suit hyperscale data center” within the Chicago metropolitan area that has been fully leased to CoreWeave for 15 years, representing around $2.2 billion in revenue, said a person with direct knowledge of the matter. CoreWeave also has two renewal options of seven years each, exercisable only with respect to the entire premises and critical IT power.

Under the lease, CoreWeave is responsible for substantially all operating expenses, including power, utilities and taxes, according to the person.

Net proceeds from the offering will be used to refinance $500 million of existing debt and accrued interest, fund the remaining estimated construction costs to complete the facility, among other things, according to the person. Any remaining net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes. The principal amount of the notes will amortize on a semi-annual basis.

Prime will guarantee the funds necessary to ensure the completion of the facility in the event that proceeds of the notes and other available funds are insufficient to do so, the person said.

Representatives for CoreWeave and Prime did not return requests for comment.

Banco Santander SA is running the sale, said the person, who asked not to be identified as discussions are private.

AI has been expanding at a breakneck pace, forcing companies to tap every corner of the US debt market to find new capacity to finance data center space, graphics-processing chips and power. Companies tied to the buildout have raised nearly $27 billion from riskier bonds so far this year, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

Click here to see Bloomberg’s AI Infrastructure Debt Monitor.

CoreWeave, part of a group of businesses known as neoclouds, leases access to AI data center capacity to clients including OpenAI, Meta Platforms Inc. and Microsoft Corp.

(Updates with details in paragraphs three to five.)

Story Continues

title: Arm Holdings Surges 14% as Nvidia Bets Its Next Chips on Arm Silicon
detail:
Arm Holdings Surges 14% as Nvidia Bets Its Next Chips on Arm Silicon
Renato Neves, CFA
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 10:37 AM MST 1 min read
ARM
NVDA
MSFT
2454.TW
HPQ
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) surged 14.35% intraday after Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) unveiled the RTX Spark Superchip and the Vera data center processor at Computex in Taipei, both built on Arm architecture. RTX Spark, co-designed with MediaTek, targets premium Windows PCs with more than 30 laptop and desktop designs from Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Dell (NYSE:DELL), HP (NYSE:HPQ), Asus, Lenovo, and MSI launching this fall. Vera is set to ship in Q3 2026. Every chip sold adds to Arm's royalty stream.

The Computex announcements landed against a strong earnings backdrop. Arm reported Q4 FY2026 results on May 6, with revenue of $1.49 billion, up 20% year-over-year, and non-GAAP EPS of $0.60. Licensing revenue climbed 29% to $819 million, royalty revenue rose 11% to $671 million, and data center royalties more than doubled year-over-year. CEO Rene Haas disclosed that committed customer demand for Arm's AGI CPU had grown to over $2 billion across fiscal 2027 and 2028, double the figure cited at launch. Full-year FY2026 revenue reached $4.92 billion, up 23%.

Arm's royalty model means it collects on every chip shipped, so a broader Arm silicon footprint across PCs and data centers is a direct revenue tailwind. The company has set a target of $15 billion in AGI CPU revenue by FY2031, with the broader CPU market opportunity estimated at over $100 billion by 2030. Arm shares are up 240% in the last three months.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Sign with DELL.

title: IREN’s Massive GPU Bet And Digital Twin Raise Execution Questions For Investors
detail:
IREN’s Massive GPU Bet And Digital Twin Raise Execution Questions For Investors
Simply Wall St
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 10:11 AM MST 3 min read
IREN
MSFT
NVDA
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

IREN (NasdaqGS:IREN) is partnering with BE Networks to use NVIDIA DSX Air to digitally simulate and validate an upcoming deployment of more than 50,000 NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs.

The partnership aims to create a digital twin of IREN's planned AI infrastructure before physical rollout to help reduce integration and deployment risk.

IREN has closed a US$3.65b investment grade GPU financing facility, described as the first of its kind in the U.S.

The financing is tied to IREN's AI cloud contract with Microsoft and is intended to support the build out of its GPU infrastructure.

IREN, listed on the NasdaqGS:IREN, has been repositioning itself around large scale AI infrastructure, with GPUs at the center of its offering. The partnership with BE Networks and use of NVIDIA's DSX Air tool fit into this shift by focusing on practical deployment issues such as configuration, integration and uptime in very large GPU clusters.

For you as an investor, the combination of a digital twin approach and a US$3.65b investment grade facility indicates that IREN is committing significant resources to its AI cloud plans with Microsoft and intends to address operational risk upfront. How the company executes on this rollout, manages financing obligations and converts capacity into contracted AI workloads is likely to be an important area to monitor over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for IREN by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on IREN.

NasdaqGS:IREN Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Jun 2026

2 things going right for IREN that this headline doesn't cover.

⚖️ Simply Wall St Valuation: The valuation status is listed as unknown, so treat IREN as a higher uncertainty case until a clear Fair Value estimate is available.

✅ Recent Momentum: A 30 day return of 39.2% shows strong recent momentum that may already reflect some of the AI and Microsoft related news.

There is only one way to know the right time to buy, sell or hold IREN. Head to the Simply Wall St company report for the latest analysis of IREN's Fair Value..

Key Considerations

📊 The digital twin work with BE Networks and NVIDIA DSX Air is aimed at reducing deployment issues as IREN rolls out more than 50,000 Blackwell Ultra GPUs for its AI infrastructure.

📊 Watch how the US$3.65b GPU financing, the Microsoft AI cloud contract and the current P/E of about 144 interact with future revenue, earnings and cash generation.

⚠️ Major risks include shareholder dilution over the past year and debt that is not well covered by operating cash flow, which both matter more as leverage rises to fund GPU build out.

title: GRAIL, Inc. (GRAL): One of the Best Stock Picks of Jeff Bezos
detail:
GRAIL, Inc. (GRAL): One of the Best Stock Picks of Jeff Bezos
Usman Kabir
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 10:11 AM MST 2 min read
MSFT
ILMN
GRAL
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

We just covered the Jeff Bezos Stock Portfolio: Top 10 Stock Picks and GRAIL, Inc. (NASDAQ:GRAL) ranks 8th on this list.

The interest of Jeff Bezos in GRAIL, Inc. (NASDAQ:GRAL) can be understood in context of the history of Bezos as a backer of disruptive, foundational healthcare platforms that use data computing. In January 2016, Bezos Expeditions joined Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates and sequencing giant Illumina to seed GRAIL’s initial $100 million launch round. The startup’s ultimate objective was to develop a holy grail diagnostic tool: a multi-cancer early detection liquid biopsy test. GRAIL’s flagship product, Galleri, utilizes advanced machine learning and next-generation sequencing to scan cell-free DNA in a single blood draw, detecting over 50 types of cancer before symptoms appear.

Jeff Bezos Investments in 2024: Companies Bezos Is Investing In

The bull case for GRAIL, Inc. (NASDAQ:GRAL) is anchored by its rapid commercial adoption curve and expanding insurance reimbursement coverage for its revolutionary Galleri multi-cancer diagnostic test. According to performance data, GRAIL has achieved significant volume growth, with hundreds of thousands of commercial tests delivered to health systems, employer programs, and concierge medical networks in Q1 2026. The company’s core operational moat lies in its vast, proprietary genomic dataset, which consistently improves the test’s positive predictive value through continuous machine learning refinement. Financially, as GRAIL scales its high-throughput laboratory infrastructure, unit economics are improving drastically, setting a clear path toward gross margin expansion.

While we acknowledge the potential of GRAL as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Stock Market Today, June 1: Markets Flat as Oil Spike Offsets New Nvidia Chip
detail:
Stock Market Today, June 1: Markets Flat as Oil Spike Offsets New Nvidia Chip
Emma Newbery, The Motley Fool
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 10:02 AM MST 2 min read
NVDA
NOW
DELL
QCOM
^DJI
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

At midday, the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX:^GSPC) inched up 0.07% to 7,585.26, the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX:^IXIC) rose 0.23% to 27,034.50 on artificial intelligence (AI) strength. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES:^DJI) slipped 0.24% to 50,912.06 as oil-linked cyclicals lagged.

Market movers

Nvidia jumped more than 4% this morning, after unveiling a new AI-focused PC chip. The news helped push Dell Technologies up 8% and Microsoft up 2%, as both firms benefit from the chip. Dell also got a boost from a Morgan Stanley upgrade, as analysts more than doubled the stock’s price target.

On the other hand, Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) and Intel fell on fears of tougher PC competition. ServiceNow extended its rally with a 10% surge as investors reassessed software stocks.

What this means for investors

Markets were muted this morning as hopes for an imminent end to the U.S.-Iran war began to fade again, even as fresh announcements from Nvidia boosted mega-cap tech stocks. Mixed reports on peace talks sent oil prices soaring, pressuring consumer cyclicals and industrials.

A Bank of America strategist drew parallels between May’s record stock performance and the peak of the dot.com bubble in 2000. Although the S&P 500 reached record levels last month, just 21 stocks in the index set new highs. Similarly, in March 2000 — the height of the internet boom — 20 stocks set records, raising concerns about extreme market concentration.

For perspective, analysts have been flagging similar issues for some time, and the AI rally continues. However, investors might revisit their exposure to AI stocks and ensure that defensive stocks such as consumer staples and healthcare are also represented.

title: Microsoft AI Push Could Lift Stock
detail:
Microsoft AI Push Could Lift Stock
Faizan Farooque
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 9:42 AM MST 1 min read
MSFT
Trade MSFT on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Microsoft (MSFT, Financials) is drawing fresh optimism as analysts point to the company's growing in-house AI strategy as a possible driver of new stock highs.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with MSFT.

Is MSFT fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The bullish view centers on Microsoft's ability to build AI deeper into products it already controls, including Azure, Office, Windows and Copilot.

That matters because Microsoft does not need to create a new customer base for AI. It already has millions of business users who rely on its software every day.

If Microsoft can turn those users into paying AI customers, the revenue opportunity could be meaningful.

The company has also been spending heavily on data centers and AI infrastructure, giving it more control over how quickly it can scale new tools.

For investors, the appeal is simple: Microsoft has the platform, the customers and the balance sheet to keep pushing AI across its business.

The next test will be whether AI demand shows up clearly in revenue growth and helps push the stock beyond its recent highs.

title: ARM Holdings Rockets 18%, Microsoft, Dell Rise on RTX Spark AI PC Launch
detail:
ARM Holdings Rockets 18%, Microsoft, Dell Rise on RTX Spark AI PC Launch
David Moadel
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 9:31 AM MST 4 min read
DELL
MSFT
NVDA
ARM
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure
Quick Read

Arm Holdings (ARM) stock jumped to $416 after NVIDIA’s (NVDA) RTX Spark Superchip validated Arm architecture for AI PCs, with the chip earning Arm royalties on every unit sold using its instruction set.

Microsoft (MSFT) stock is up 2% as RTX Spark runs Windows on Arm, delivering Microsoft’s highest-profile AI PC silicon validation yet.

Dell Technologies (DELL) stock is rising 8% as Dell is an early launch OEM partner for RTX Spark systems debuting this fall, following Q1 revenue of $43.84B (up 88% YoY) and $24.4B in AI orders booked.

The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and Arm wasn't one of them. Get them here FREE.

Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) stock is ripping higher in midday trading on Monday, June 1, jumping 18% to around $416 after NVIDIA's (NASDAQ:NVDA) RTX Spark Superchip launch validated the Arm architecture as the foundation for the next wave of AI PCs. The move builds on an already extraordinary run.

ARM stock closed Friday at $353.29 and entered today carrying a 223% year to date (YTD) gain. Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stock is up 2% and Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) stock is up 8% as both ride the same catalyst.

The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 stocks and Arm wasn't one of them. Get them here FREE.

RTX Spark Validates Arm for AI PCs

RTX Spark was unveiled at Computex 2026 and is co-developed with MediaTek. The chip runs Microsoft's Windows on Arm operating system, putting Arm architecture at the heart of NVIDIA's first mainstream AI PC platform.

Arm earns royalties on every chip built using its instruction set, so a flagship AI PC silicon win flows directly to the licensing line. RTX Spark systems will debut this fall in Dell and Lenovo laptops and desktops.

Microsoft Gains a Flagship Windows on Arm Partner

Microsoft has been pushing Windows on Arm for years, and RTX Spark gives the platform its highest-profile silicon partner yet. The timing dovetails with the company's broader AI surge. Microsoft's AI revenue is up 123% year over year (YoY) and commercial remaining performance obligation reached $627 billion, up 99%.

Reddit sentiment on MSFT stock has skewed bullish, with one widely circulated r/WallStreetBets post titled "€220k into Microsoft because in 5 years everyone will pretend this was obvious" drawing more than 1,100 upvotes. MSFT shares trade at a P/E ratio of 33x, modest relative to the AI cohort.

Dell Lands as a Launch OEM

Dell is one of the first OEM partners for RTX Spark-powered PCs, and the timing follows blockbuster Q1 FY2027 results. Dell posted revenue of $43.84 billion, up 88% YoY, with AI-Optimized Servers revenue of $16.13 billion and $24.4 billion in AI orders booked in the quarter. Full results are available in Dell's SEC filings.

title: NVIDIA Rallies 5% on RTX Spark Launch as Qualcomm Falls 7% on AI PC Competition Fears
detail:
NVIDIA Rallies 5% on RTX Spark Launch as Qualcomm Falls 7% on AI PC Competition Fears
David Moadel
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 8:54 AM MST 3 min read
NVDA
QCOM
2454.TW
MSFT
AMD
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure
Quick Read

NVIDIA (NVDA) unveiled the RTX Spark superchip at Computex 2026, a chip co-developed with MediaTek running Windows on Arm (ARM) that directly competes with Qualcomm’s (QCOM) Snapdragon X PC franchise, sending shares to $222.

Qualcomm (QCOM) shares fell to $234 as RTX Spark targets the exact same PC market socket, though Qualcomm’s automotive segment grew 38% year-over-year to $1.33 billion and the company reported hyperscaler custom silicon shipments launching later this year.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

Shares of NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) are up 5% in midday trading Monday, changing hands near $222 after the chipmaker unveiled its RTX Spark superchip at Computex 2026. The rally lifts NVDA stock well above Friday's $211 close.

The opposite story is playing out at Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM). QCOM stock is down 7% to roughly $234, with an intraday low of $227, as traders price in a direct hit to its Snapdragon X PC franchise. The setup is a clean winner-loser pairing inside the AI PC chip race, with NVIDIA's Computex blitz absorbing the spotlight and Qualcomm's own data-center brand launch getting pushed to the side.

RTX Spark Targets Windows on Arm

RTX Spark is a new NVIDIA superchip co-developed with MediaTek that runs Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) Windows on Arm's (NASDAQ:ARM) operating system. NVIDIA's chip is a direct challenge Qualcomm in the Windows PC market.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang's framing on the most recent earnings call still applies. He called the current cycle "the buildout of AI factories, the largest infrastructure expansion in human history," and RTX Spark extends that pitch onto the desk and into the laptop bag.

Qualcomm's Dragonfly Gets Overshadowed

Qualcomm used Computex to unveil Dragonfly, its new AI data-center brand, and CEO Cristiano Amon pitched a vision for agentic AI with a 40x AI token surge by 2030. Stocktwits described the result bluntly, saying Qualcomm's push was "overshadowed by Nvidia's Computex blitz."

The competitive math is straightforward. Snapdragon X has been the anchor for Windows on Arm PCs, and RTX Spark now drops into the exact same socket with NVIDIA's GPU brand power attached. Qualcomm shares had run hard into the event, leaving little cushion when the headline risk hit.

However, the selloff is being read as competitive pressure rather than a thesis break. Qualcomm's automotive segment grew 38% year over year last quarter to a record $1.33 billion, and management has flagged a hyperscaler custom silicon engagement with initial shipments later this calendar year.

title: Microsoft shares rise as Citizens starts coverage with  bullish AI outlook
detail:
Microsoft shares rise as Citizens starts coverage with bullish AI outlook
Jaiveer Shekhawat
Mon, June 1, 2026 at 8:47 AM MST 2 min read
MSFT
Trade MSFT on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Investing.com --Shares of Microsoft were higher on Monday after Citizens JMP Securities initiated coverage with a Market Outperform rating.

The brokerage firm put a $550 price target, citing the company's strong positioning in enterprise AI and attractive valuation following a recent pullback.

Citizens said that the software giant is well positioned to capitalize on the emerging era of enterprise AI despite recent investor concerns over its reliance on third-party models and heavy infrastructure spending.

Citizens estimates Microsoft's total addressable market could expand from roughly $1.8 trillion in 2025 to $5.1 trillion by 2030, driven by growth in AI software, infrastructure, cybersecurity, and enterprise applications. The firm believes Microsoft's broad enterprise footprint, Azure cloud platform, security capabilities, and extensive partner ecosystem position it to capture a significant share of that opportunity.

The report forecasts revenue growth accelerating to 17% in fiscal 2026 from 15% in fiscal 2025, with operating margins expanding to 47%. Citizens projects fiscal 2026 revenue of $329.5 billion and non-GAAP earnings per share of $16.81, broadly in line with Wall Street expectations.

The brokerage also pointed to strong momentum in Azure, which it estimates is running at an annual revenue pace exceeding $100 billion and continuing to benefit from AI-related demand. Meanwhile, Microsoft's growing AI portfolio—including Copilot, Foundry, Fabric, and custom silicon initiatives such as Maia and Cobalt—should strengthen its competitive position across the AI stack.

However, Citizens cautioned that risks remain, including increased competition from AI-native companies, uncertainty around AI monetization and pricing models, rising capital expenditures tied to AI infrastructure, and Microsoft's continued exposure to OpenAI-related commitments.

At the current share price of about $450, Citizens argues Microsoft trades at a discount to large-cap technology peers on a 2027 earnings basis, leaving room for multiple expansion as investors gain confidence in the company's long-term AI strategy.
